steps.title: Locate the correct age group and year
steps.content: The question asks for Girl Scouts members aged 14–17 in 1995. Looking at the table, “Seniors (14–17 years)” is the only row matching that age range, and we need the column labeled 1995.

steps.title: Read the value at the intersection
steps.content: The cell where “Seniors (14–17 years)” meets the 1995 column shows 52 (in thousands). The other choices either come from different rows or years—727 is from Juniors in 1995, and 45 is from Seniors but in 1994—making them incorrect selections. B is correct.
